title: Buttermilk waffles
subtitle: Crisp American-style waffles made with buttermilk
excerpt: Buttermilk waffles made from a straightforward batter with baking powder and baking soda. They come out of the iron crisp and golden on the outside and tender within. The buttermilk adds a slight tang. Serve immediately while the steam still rises.
sourceNotes: Buttermilk waffles are a documented American breakfast tradition with roots in the waffle houses of the American South. Buttermilk-leavened batters, where the acid of the buttermilk reacts with bicarbonate of soda to produce lift, have been central to American Southern baking since the nineteenth century. The recipe follows the standard American domestic buttermilk waffle format.

recipe.freezeNotes: Cool on a rack and freeze individually. Reheat in a toaster or hot oven for a crisp result.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The batter can be made up to 1 hour ahead and left at room temperature. Do not make further ahead or the leavening loses its activity.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: cooked in a waffle iron on a medium-high setting

glossaryTerms.definition: Buttermilk waffle batter uses two leaveners: baking powder, which reacts on contact with moisture and again with heat; and bicarbonate of soda, which reacts with the acid in the buttermilk. Together they produce a lighter, crisper result than either alone. Do not overmix the batter after the wet ingredients go in, as this knocks out the carbon dioxide bubbles before they can set in the iron.

content.text: The rule with waffle batter is to stop mixing while it still looks lumpy. A smooth batter is an overmixed batter. The
provides the lift. Leave it to work and do not stir again once the batter rests.

Heat the waffle iron. Whisk together 200 g plain flour, 2 tsp baking powder, 0.5 tsp bicarbonate of soda, 1 tbsp caster sugar, and 3 g salt in a large bowl. In a separate bowl or jug, whisk 2 eggs with 300 ml buttermilk, 60 g cooled melted butter, and 1 tsp vanilla extract. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and stir until just combined. The batter should have lumps. Do not overmix. Leave to stand for 5 minutes. Pour enough batter into the waffle iron to just fill the plates. Close and cook for 4 to 5 minutes until steam stops escaping and the waffle is golden and crisp. Transfer to a rack immediately; do not stack or they will steam each other soft. Repeat with remaining batter.

items.problem: Waffles are soft and do not crisp
items.solution: The iron was not hot enough, or the waffles were stacked while hot and steamed each other soft. Make sure the iron is fully preheated. Cook until steam stops coming from the sides of the iron, then give it another 30 seconds.
The batter was too lean, or the iron needed oiling. Brush the plates lightly with melted butter or cooking spray between waffles if sticking occurs.
